title: Extracellular vesicle isolation from bacterial cultures
author: Steven Biller
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.8fzhtp6

steps.instruction: Grow bacterial culture to mid/late exponential phase

Remove cells by filtration through a 0.2µm filter and collect the filtrate (<0.2µm fraction). Depending on the culture, the bulk cell mass can be first removed by gentle centrifugation (~10,000 xg or less) prior to filtration. Whatman Polycap TC 0.2 µm capsule filter (capsule filter, Whatman, 6717-9502)
Concentrate <0.2µm, cell-free supernatant using a tangential flow filter (100 kDa cutoff). Try to keep feed pressure <10 psi. Try to get the final volume as low as possible.
Re-filter concentrated material through a 0.2 µm syringe filter. Pellet the vesicles in an ultracentrifuge at 100,000 xg, for at least 1 hr, at 10 C or lower.

A pellet will not necessarily be visible. Remove as much of the supernatant as possible. If desired, wash the vesicle pellet in the appropriate buffer (media, 1x PBS, etc) as needed for downstream application.
Resuspend final vesicle pellet in buffer. Store at -20 or -80 C.
